input.title: Peptide YY administration and satiety: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized trials
input.abstract: We conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials evaluating exogenous PYY(3-36) on energy intake and appetite ratings in healthy adults. Twelve eligible RCTs (n=287 unique participants across crossover trials; total observations=412) were identified via PubMed and EMBASE searches. Intravenous or subcutaneous PYY(3-36) reduced ad libitum energy intake by a pooled mean of −178 kcal (95% CI: −241 to −115; I²=42%) compared with saline. Appetite visual-analogue-scale scores were also significantly reduced (SMD −0.61; 95% CI: −0.88 to −0.34). Nausea was the most common adverse effect (24% vs 6% placebo). Funnel-plot asymmetry was modest. PYY(3-36) robustly suppresses appetite and energy intake in the short term.
